title: Crimson Desert is so packed with weird systems and quirks that it can be a struggle to remember them all—which is why we've made a quiz about everything from the stock market to space
summary: Did you even make it out of Hernand yet to know these?
body: Crimson Desert is a ridiculous game with a ridiculous number of ridiculous things you can do. So much so that it's hard to believe some of those things are actually real. Like what, I hear you ask? Well, if I told you, that would spoil the lovely quiz I've put together to test just how well you know Crimson Desert's bizarre features (or lack thereof). More quizzes! (Image credit: Larian Studios, PC Gamer) Want to keep testing your knowledge of gaming trivia? We've got loads more PC Gamer quizzes , on everything from healthbars to weird currencies to absurd patch notes. Is your mount knowledge up to scratch? Do you know just how much of the world Pywel's huge map truly covers? Are you properly clued up on all of Crimson Desert's little quirks and nuances that make it the slightly alien, loveable game we've all sunk far too many hours into since it released? Are you guys even done exploring Hernand yet? Either way, I've lined up 10 multiple choice questions for you that'll test just how much you know about this game—or at least how much you've been reading our lovely writers' stories about all of the weird stuff you can (or can't) do. Even some of our more veteran Greymanes on the t…

title: Space-obsessed Pragmata actor was so wary of breaking embargo that he refused to post about Artemis 2: 'I know so much about the f**king moon'
summary: “My entire career has been around the moon."
body: Videogames love pitting players against the moon or otherwise convincing them it's a big problem , and while I loved Pragmata , it only saddled me with more lunar trauma. David Menkin, the actor who played protagonist Hugh, has always loved the big grey rock, but as he mentioned in an interview with Polygon , he had to keep his excitement for the recent Artemis 2 mission under wraps while he was working on Capcom's shooter. It's not that the embargo specifically forbade him from talking about the moon, but he wanted to post about the coincidence that Artemis 2 and Pragmata's release date were so close together. The full suite of embargoes and restrictions was such a tangle, and Menkin is such a self-described "bad liar," that he felt he had to "fully boot down" so as to not let slip any top secret info. “I didn't want anyone from Capcom to be like, ‘Excuse me, is that a picture of the moon or is that a picture of our moon?’” he told Polygon. The article points out that Menkin has a lot of experience playing a spacefarer, whether it's Luke Skywalker in Lego Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga or Neil Armstrong, who he's played in both a short film called Moths to Flame and an audio serie…
